segments.text: "When Samuel grew old, he made his sons leaders over Israel.
"His first son was named Joel, and his second son was named Abijah. They were rulers in Beersheba.
"However, his sons did not follow his ways. They were corrupt, making money by accepting bribes and perverting justice.
"So the elders of Israel all joined together and came to meet Samuel at Ramah.
Look here,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they told him,
you are old, and your sons do not follow your ways. Choose a king to rule over us like all the other nations.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: "Samuel thought this was a bad idea when they said,
Give us a king to rule over us,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: so he prayed to the Lord about it.
Do what the people tell you,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: the Lord said to Samuel,
because it's not you they're rejecting, but me as their king.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: They're doing just as they have always done since I led them out of Egypt right up to now. They have abandoned me and worshiped other gods, and they're doing just the same to you.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: So do what they want, but give them a solemn warning—explain to them what a king will do when he rules over them.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: "Samuel repeated all that the Lord had said to the people asking him to give them a king.
This is what a king will do when he rules over you,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he told them.
He will take your sons and make them serve as charioteers and horsemen, and to run as a guard in front of his chariot.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Some of them he will make commanders of thousands and commanders of fifties, and some will have to plow his fields and reap his harvest. Some will be assigned to making weapons of war and chariot equipment.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He will take your daughters and have them work as perfume-makers, cooks, and bakers.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He will take your best fields, vineyards, and olive groves and give them to his officials.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He will take a tenth of your grain harvest and the produce of your vineyards and allocate it to his chief officers and officials.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He will take your male servants and your female servants, and your best young men and your donkeys, and make them work for him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He will take a tenth of your flocks, and you yourselves will be his slaves.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: On that day you will plead to be rescued from the king you have chosen, but the Lord won't answer you then.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: "But the people refused to listen to what Samuel said.
No!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they insisted.
We want our own king!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: That way we can be like all the other nations. Our king will rule us, and will lead us when we go out to fight our battles.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: "Samuel listened to everything the people said and repeated it to the Lord.
"The Lord told Samuel,
Do what they say and give them a king.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Then Samuel told the Israelites,
Go back to your homes.
segments.speaker: Samuel
